content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 1, 2001

Kathleen M. Weinheimer
Attorney at Law
420 Alameda Padre Serra
Santa Barbara, CA 93103

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. 1-01-239

Dear Ms. Weinheimer:

This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You are requesting advice regarding a possible conflict of interest involving Gail Marshall, a member of the Santa Barbara County Board of Supervisors, and the Santa Barbara Flower Growers Association. While the Act requires that the Commission provide formal written advice or informal assistance to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative, the Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public official's duties under the Act. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) Furthermore, your questions involve issues of past conduct, and the Commission does not provide formal advice or informal assistance regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329.) Therefore, we decline to provide the advice you have requested.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-01-239
Page No. 2

If you believe that a violation of the Act has occurred, you may file a complaint
with the Commission's Enforcement Division. I have enclosed brochures explaining the
complaint procedure and a complaint form. Please contact the Enforcement Division at
(800) 561-1861 if you have any questions about the complaint procedure.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kelly L. Winsor
Legal Analyst

LM:KLW:jg

Enclosures: Regulation 18329
Complaint Procedure and Form
"What happens after I file a complaint with the FPPC?"

I:\AdviceLtrs\01-239

sections.conclusion: The Commission declines to provide the advice requested because: (1) the Commission does not provide advice to third parties about a public official's duties under the Act, and (2) the questions involve issues of past conduct, for which the Commission does not provide formal advice or informal assistance.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought advice regarding a potential conflict of interest involving a Santa Barbara County Board of Supervisors member and the Santa Barbara Flower Growers Association.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide informal assistance because the requester is a third party (not the public official in question) and the inquiry concerns past conduct, both of which are outside the scope of the Commission's advisory authority.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the document. The legal question and conclusion were synthesized from the letter's narrative content. The date on the document is October 1, 2007, but the file number indicates 2001; this appears to be correspondence from 2007 regarding a 2001 file number.

QUESTION: The requester sought advice regarding a potential conflict of interest involving a Santa Barbara County Board of Supervisors member and the Santa Barbara Flower Growers Association.

CONCLUSION: The Commission declines to provide the advice requested because: (1) the Commission does not provide advice to third parties about a public official's duties under the Act, and (2) the questions involve issues of past conduct, for which the Commission does not provide formal advice or informal assistance.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for informal assistance regarding a potential conflict of interest involving a Santa Barbara County supervisor, citing that the Commission does not advise third parties and does not provide guidance on past conduct. The FPPC directed the requester to file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if a violation is believed to have occurred.
